Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories Adn For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,150.00 Against Defendant Rodrick Fods Inc Dba Mcdonald'S
Off Calendar May-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CARLETTA JACKSON-LANE'S Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,150.00 Against Defendant Rodrick Fods Inc Dba Mcdonald'S

Plaintiff's complaint alleges she suffered injuries in a slip and fall accident on April 26, 2022 on the premises of the defendant's fast food restaurant in San Francisco. Plaintiff alleges that the premises were not adequately maintained such that she fell due to a foreign substance on the surface of the restaurant's parking lot.

On or about November 21, 2023, plaintiff served defendant with a set of special interrogatories, a request for admissions and a request for production of documents, with responses due on January 5, 2024. According to the defendant, three extensions of the due date were agreed to by the parties, resulting in defendant's responses to the discovery being due on February 9, 2024. Defendant's counsel contends timely responses to the three discovery efforts were indeed timely served, and there is no denial by plaintiff of that assertion. Instead, in three motions to compel, one each for the special interrogatories, request for admissions and request for production of documents, plaintiff asserts that defendant's responses to discovery were inadequate. It is not clear whether plaintiff contends those responses were untimely, although there is reference here and there to a claim that defendant waived objections, presumably for failing to respond in a timely manner. Although the court file contains copies of defendant's responses to the three discovery forms, those copies do not include a proof of service showing their service no later than February 9, 2024. On the basis of the record now before the Court, it assumes service of those responses was timely.
Given that fact, Rule 3.1345(a)(1), (2) and (3) require that when a motion to compel discovery is filed, it be accompanied by a separate statement in compliance with subsection (c) of that rule. Plaintiff did not file a separate statement in support of any of its three motions. Nor does the file contain any showing that plaintiff initiated a good faith meet and confer with defendant with respect to the adequacy of its amended discovery responses.

For these reasons, plaintiff has not complied with governing rules of court and controlling provisions of the Discovery Act. Accordingly, and for good cause shown, plaintiff's three motions to compel are DENIED. Also DENIED is her request for an award of sanctions. In that regard, the Court suggests counsel for plaintiff update references in her pleadings to the Rules of Court. Former Rule 341(a) is now Rule 3.1348(a).

Counsel for defendant is directed to prepare a proposed form of order verbatim to the Court's ruling by email at psw@hassard.com. Submitted proposed order to comply with CRC 3.1312, Approval as to Form.

Judge Pro Tem: Phillip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
